response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems engineer, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, plus a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development. C++ does not even appear in his skills section. He has no demonstrated experience on Windows as a primary development platform, no exposure to Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking systems, and no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle work. His career trajectory is firmly rooted in TypeScript/Node.js web development, with detours into DLang and mobile — valuable skills, but orthogonal to what this role demands.
strengths: Solid CI/CD and DevOps fundamentals (GitLab, Docker, CI servers, trunk-based development) align with desirable skills
Java experience mentioned across multiple roles, satisfying one optional requirement
Strong communication skills evidenced by public talks, full-day workshops, and course creation
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, a decade ago) against a mandatory 5–10 year requirement; C++ is absent from his own skills list
Zero domain exposure to Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL appears only as a generic skill line item with no depth described
